Coagulation

Test01-OCT-2017Reference RangeUnit

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows marked hyperbilirubinemia and elevated creatinine, indicating significant liver dysfunction and renal impairment. Severe acidemia (pH 6.9) and low total CO₂ suggest metabolic acidosis. Hemoglobin is mildly elevated, and LDL cholesterol is slightly high. Other parameters are within normal limits. Immediate clinical evaluation is recommended due to life-threatening acid-base disturbance and multi-organ dysfunction.
